source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the --client-cert-auth argument is set to true

input_row.source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure that the --client-cert-auth argument is set to true
input_row.description: Enable	client	authentication	on	etcd	service.
input_row.rationale: etcd	is	a	highly-available	key	value	store	used	by	Kubernetes	deployments	for	persistent storage	of	all	of	its	REST	API	objects.	These	objects	are	sensitive	in	nature	and	should	not be	available	to	unauthenticated	clients.	You	should	enable	the	client	authentication	via valid	certificates	to	secure	the	access	to	the	etcd	service. Impact: All	clients	attempting	to	access	the	etcd	server	will	require	a	valid	client	certificate.
input_row.audit: Run	the	following	command	on	the	etcd	server	node: ps -ef | grep etcd Verify	that	the	--client-cert-auth	argument	is	set	to	true.
input_row.remediation: Edit	the	etcd	pod	specification	file	/etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml	on	the	master node	and	set	the	below	parameter. --client-cert-auth="true" Default	Value: By	default,	the	etcd	service	can	be	queried	by	unauthenticated	clients. References: 1. https://coreos.com/etcd/docs/latest/op-guide/security.html 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/etcd/ 142	|	P a g e 3. https://coreos.com/etcd/docs/latest/op-guide/configuration.html#client-cert-auth Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 14	Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know 143	|	P a g e
input_row.impact: All	clients	attempting	to	access	the	etcd	server	will	require	a	valid	client	certificate.
input_row.references: 1. https://coreos.com/etcd/docs/latest/op-guide/security.html 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/etcd/ 142	|	P a g e 3. https://coreos.com/etcd/docs/latest/op-guide/configuration.html#client-cert-auth Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 14	Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know 143	|	P a g e

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**

- This control deals with the configuration of the etcd server within a Kubernetes environment, specifically relating to a command-line argument (`--client-cert-auth`) that is set during the server's initialization.
- The audit process requires checking a running process on the server node, which is indicative that it requires SSH access to the server to execute shell commands.
- Given that this is a configuration aspect at the pod specification and requires command-line access to verify, the audit cannot be performed using Alibaba Cloud's available APIs or CLI tools which manage Kubernetes cluster settings.
- The audit instruction specifies the use of `ps -ef | grep etcd` on an etcd server node, which is a manual, non-API, command-line check.

**MANUAL STEPS:**

1. Gain SSH access to the master node of the Kubernetes cluster where the etcd service is running.
2. Run the following command to inspect the process arguments for the etcd service:
   ```bash
   ps -ef | grep etcd
   ```
3. Review the output to locate the `--client-cert-auth` argument.
4. Verify that the `--client-cert-auth` argument is set to `true`.
5. If the argument is not set or set to `false`, the configuration is non-compliant and remediation steps should be followed to modify the etcd pod specification file located typically at `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml` on the master node to include `--client-cert-auth="true"`.
